titleStmt.title: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in France ( Caffery )

div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in France ( Caffery )
div.p: 1949. Following telegram dated May 8 received from the Secretary at San Francisco,The United Nations Conference on International Organization met at San Francisco from April 25 to June 26, 1945. is repeated for your information.

quote.p: “The subject of Indo-China came up in a recent conversation I had with BidaultGeorges Bidault, French Minister for Foreign Affairs. and Bonnet. The latter remarked that although the French Government interprets Mr. Welles’ statement of 1942For text of note dated April 13, 1942, from Acting Secretary of State Sumner Welles to Ambassador Gaston Henry-Haye, see Foreign Relations, 1942, vol. ii, p. 561, or Department of State Bulletin, April 18, 1942, p. 335. concerning the restoration of French sovereignty over the French Empire as including Indo-China, the press continues to imply that a special status will be reserved for this colonial area. It was made quite clear to Bidault that the record is entirely innocent of any official statement of this government questioning, even by implication, French sovereignty over Indo-China. Certain elements of American public opinion, however, condemned French governmental policies and practices in Indo-China. Bidault seemed relieved and has no doubt cabled Paris that he received renewed assurances of our recognition of French sovereignty over that area.”
